rawText:
President Faust, Board of Overseers, faculty, alumni, friends, proud parents, members of the ad board, and graduates of the greatest university in the world,

I'm honored to be with you today because, let's face it, you accomplished something I never could. If I get through this speech, it'll be the first time I actually finish something at Harvard. Class of 2017, congratulations!

I'm an unlikely speaker, not just because I dropped out, but because we're technically in the same generation. We walked this yard less than a decade apart, studied the same ideas and slept through the same Ec10 lectures. We may have taken different paths to get here, especially if you came all the way from the Quad, but today I want to share what I've learned about our generation and the world we're building together.

But first, the last couple of days have brought back a lot of good memories.

How many of you remember exactly what you were doing when you got that email telling you that you got into Harvard? I was playing Civilization and I ran downstairs, got my dad, and for some reason, his reaction was to video me opening the email. That could have been a really sad video. I swear getting into Harvard is still the thing my parents are most proud of me for.

What about your first lecture at Harvard? Mine was Computer Science 121 with the incredible Harry Lewis. I was late so I threw on a t-shirt and didn't realize until afterwards it was inside out and backwards with my tag sticking out the front. I couldn't figure out why no one would talk to me — except one guy, KX Jin, he just went with it. We ended up doing our problem sets together, and now he runs a big part of Facebook. And that, Class of 2017, is why you should be nice to people.

But my best memory from Harvard was meeting Priscilla. I had just launched this prank website Facemash, and the ad board wanted to "see me". Everyone thought I was going to get kicked out. My parents came to help me pack. My friends threw me a going away party. As luck would have it, Priscilla was at that party with her friend. We met in line for the bathroom in the Pfoho Belltower, and in what must be one of the all time romantic lines, I said: "I'm going to get kicked out in three days, so we need to go on a date quickly."

Actually, any of you graduating can use that line.

I didn't end up getting kicked out — I did that to myself. Priscilla and I started dating. And, you know, that movie made it seem like Facemash was so important to creating Facebook. It wasn't. But without Facemash I wouldn't have met Priscilla, and she's the most important person in my life, so you could say it was the most important thing I built in my time here.

We've all started lifelong friendships here, and some of us even families. That's why I'm so grateful to this place. Thanks, Harvard.

Today I want to talk about purpose. But I'm not here to give you the standard commencement about finding your purpose. We're millennials. We'll try to do that instinctively. Instead, I'm here to tell you finding your purpose isn't enough. The challenge for our generation is creating a world where everyone has a sense of purpose.

sentences.original: President Faust, Board of Overseers, faculty, alumni, friends, proud parents, members of the ad board, and graduates of the greatest university in the world,
sentences.translation: 파우스트 총장님, 이사회 위원 여러분, 교수진, 동문, 친구들, 자랑스러운 부모님들, 징계위원회 위원 여러분, 그리고 세계 최고의 대학교 졸업생 여러분,

grammarElements.text: graduates of the greatest university in the world
grammarElements.label: 호격 (최상급 수식)

vocabulary.example: The Board of Overseers governs the university.
The faculty voted on the new curriculum.
The alumni donated generously to their alma mater.

expressions.usage: 하버드 대학교의 최고 의사결정 기구를 지칭할 때
하버드의 학생 징계를 담당하는 위원회. 여기서 유머러스하게 언급

patterns.explanation: 졸업식 연설의 전형적 오프닝. 청중을 직함과 그룹별로 나열하며 호명하는 격식체 인사.
examples: Mr. President, distinguished guests, faculty, and students,
Your Excellency, ministers, delegates, and honored guests,

drillQuestions.question: President Faust, Board of _____, faculty, and alumni...
The _____ of the greatest university in the world.

sentences.notes: 졸업식 연설의 격식 있는 인사. "ad board"는 Administrative Board의 줄임말로, 하버드에서 학생 징계를 담당하는 위원회. 저커버그 본인이 징계위에 불려갔던 경험을 슬쩍 암시하는 유머.

koreanAlignment.en: graduates of the greatest university in the world

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 열거법(enumeration) — 청중 그룹을 격식에 맞게 나열하여 모두를 포괄하는 개회사

sentences.original: I'm honored to be with you today because, let's face it, you accomplished something I never could.
sentences.translation: 오늘 여러분과 함께할 수 있어 영광입니다. 왜냐하면, 솔직히 말하면, 여러분은 제가 절대 해내지 못한 일을 해냈으니까요.

grammarElements.text: I'm honored to be with you today
grammarElements.label: 주절 (be honored to — ~하게 되어 영광이다)

vocabulary.example: I'm honored to receive this award.
She accomplished her goal of running a marathon.

expressions.usage: 불편하거나 솔직한 사실을 인정할 때 사용하는 관용 표현
격식 있는 자리에서 감사와 겸손을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 연설 시작 시 겸손함과 감사를 표현하는 격식체 패턴. because 이하에서 이유를 밝힘.
examples: I'm honored to speak here because this university shaped my career.
I'm honored to join you because your work has inspired millions.

drillQuestions.question: I'm _____ to be with you today.
You accomplished something I never _____.

sentences.notes: "something I never could"에서 관계대명사 that/which가 생략되어 있고, could 뒤에 accomplish가 생략되어 있다. 중퇴한 본인이 졸업생들에게 "졸업"을 해낸 것을 칭찬하는 자기비하 유머.

koreanAlignment.en: I'm honored to be with you today
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘 여러분과 함께할 수 있어 영광입니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자기비하 유머(self-deprecation) — 하버드 중퇴를 유머로 전환하여 청중의 호감을 유도

sentences.original: If I get through this speech, it'll be the first time I actually finish something at Harvard.
sentences.translation: 이 연설을 끝까지 해낸다면, 제가 하버드에서 실제로 무언가를 끝내는 건 처음일 겁니다.

grammarElements.text: If I get through this speech
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if — 1차 조건문)

grammarElements.text: I actually finish something at Harvard
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (that/when 생략)

vocabulary.example: I need to get through this report by Friday.
I actually enjoyed the movie more than I expected.

expressions.usage: 어려운 일이나 긴 과정을 완료할 때
과장스럽게 처음임을 강조하여 유머를 만들 때

patterns.pattern: If I [verb], it'll be the first time I [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 조건절로 가정을 세우고, "처음"이라는 사실을 강조하는 패턴. 유머에 자주 활용.
examples: If I pass this exam, it'll be the first time I pass without studying.
If we win today, it'll be the first time we beat this team.

drillQuestions.question: If I get _____ this speech, it'll be the first time.
It'll be the first time I actually _____ something at Harvard.

sentences.notes: "the first time"절에서 접속사 that/when이 생략. "get through"는 물리적 통과가 아닌 "끝까지 완수하다"는 의미. 하버드를 중퇴했다는 사실을 유쾌하게 활용한 펀치라인.

koreanAlignment.en: If I get through this speech

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자기비하 유머(self-deprecation) — 중퇴 사실을 연설 완주와 연결한 펀치라인

sentences.original: Class of 2017, congratulations!
sentences.translation: 2017년 졸업생 여러분, 축하합니다!

vocabulary.example: The class of 2020 had a virtual graduation.
Congratulations on your promotion!

expressions.usage: 특정 연도의 졸업생을 총칭할 때. 미국 교육 문화에서 매우 흔한 표현

patterns.explanation: 졸업식 연설에서 졸업생들에게 축하를 전하는 전형적 패턴.
examples: Class of 2024, congratulations!
Class of 2025, you made it!

drillQuestions.question: _____ of 2017, congratulations!

sentences.notes: "Class of + 연도"는 미국 학교 문화의 핵심 개념. 졸업 연도가 정체성의 일부가 되며, 동문 네트워크의 기본 단위가 된다.

sentences.original: I'm an unlikely speaker, not just because I dropped out, but because we're technically in the same generation.
sentences.translation: 저는 어울리지 않는 연사입니다. 제가 중퇴해서만이 아니라, 기술적으로 우리가 같은 세대이기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: not just because I dropped out
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (이유 — 부분 부정)

grammarElements.text: but because we're technically in the same generation
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (이유 — 진짜 이유)

vocabulary.example: She was an unlikely hero in the crisis.
He dropped out of college to start his business.
Technically, we're still in the same department.
Each generation faces unique challenges.

expressions.phrase: not just because A, but because B
expressions.meaning: A 때문만이 아니라 B 때문이기도 하다
expressions.usage: 두 가지 이유를 제시하되, 두 번째 이유를 더 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: I'm an unlikely [noun], not just because [A], but because [B]
patterns.explanation: 자신이 해당 역할에 어울리지 않는 이유를 두 가지 제시하며, 겸손하면서도 유머러스한 톤을 만드는 패턴.
examples: I'm an unlikely mentor, not just because I'm young, but because I'm still learning myself.
She's an unlikely CEO, not just because of her age, but because she never planned to lead.

drillQuestions.question: I'm an unlikely speaker, not just _____ I dropped out.
We're _____ in the same generation.

sentences.notes: "not just A but B" 구조에서 B가 진짜 강조하고 싶은 이유. "unlikely speaker"는 보통 졸업 연사가 연장자인데, 저커버그가 졸업생들과 같은 세대라는 점이 이례적임을 유머로 전달.

koreanAlignment.en: not just because I dropped out
but because we're technically in the same generation

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 겸양법(modesty topos) — 연사가 자신의 자격을 낮추어 청중과의 거리를 좁히는 고전적 수사법

sentences.original: We walked this yard less than a decade apart, studied the same ideas and slept through the same Ec10 lectures.
sentences.translation: 우리는 10년도 안 되는 차이로 이 마당을 걸었고, 같은 아이디어를 공부했으며, 같은 경제학 10번 강의 시간에 잠들었습니다.

grammarElements.text: and slept through the same Ec10 lectures
grammarElements.label: 병렬 동사구 (3)

vocabulary.example: Students gather in Harvard Yard between classes.
They graduated only two years apart.
I accidentally slept through my alarm.

expressions.usage: 두 사건이나 경험 사이의 시간적 간격이 짧음을 강조
지루한 수업에서 잠든 경험을 유머러스하게 표현

patterns.pattern: We [verb A], [verb B] the same [X], and [verb C] the same [Y]
patterns.explanation: 세 가지 병렬 동사로 공유 경험을 나열하는 패턴. 마지막이 유머인 경우 "삼단 구조 유머(rule of three)".
examples: We ate the same food, wore the same uniform, and endured the same drills.
We read the same books, debated the same topics, and complained about the same professors.

drillQuestions.question: We walked this yard less than a decade _____.
We slept _____ the same Ec10 lectures.

sentences.notes: "Harvard Yard"는 하버드 캠퍼스의 상징적 중심 공간. "Ec10"은 하버드의 유명한 입문 경제학 강의(Economics 10)의 약칭. "slept through"가 세 번째에 배치되어 삼단 유머(rule of three)를 완성.

koreanAlignment.en: and slept through the same Ec10 lectures

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼단 구조 유머(rule of three) — 두 개의 진지한 내용 뒤에 유머러스한 세 번째를 배치

sentences.original: We may have taken different paths to get here, especially if you came all the way from the Quad, but today I want to share what I've learned about our generation and the world we're building together.
sentences.translation: 우리가 여기까지 오는 데 다른 경로를 택했을 수 있습니다, 특히 여러분이 쿼드에서 먼 길을 오셨다면요. 하지만 오늘 저는 우리 세대와 우리가 함께 만들어가는 세상에 대해 제가 배운 것을 나누고 싶습니다.

grammarElements.text: We may have taken different paths
grammarElements.label: 주절 (may have + p.p. — 과거 추측)

grammarElements.text: especially if you came all the way from the Quad
grammarElements.label: 조건 삽입절 (if)

grammarElements.text: but today I want to share
grammarElements.label: 대조 주절 (but)

grammarElements.text: what I've learned about our generation
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (what — 명사절)

grammarElements.text: and the world we're building together
grammarElements.label: 등위 명사절

vocabulary.example: Everyone follows a different path in life.
Students in the Quad have a longer walk to class.

expressions.usage: 출발지로부터의 긴 거리를 강조할 때
과거의 가능성을 추측하여 말할 때

patterns.pattern: We may have [past participle] ..., but today I want to share what I've learned about ...
patterns.explanation: 과거의 차이점을 인정한 뒤, 오늘의 공유 목적으로 전환하는 연설 도입 패턴.
examples: We may have come from different backgrounds, but today I want to share what I've learned about leadership.
We may have faced different challenges, but today I want to share what I've discovered.

drillQuestions.question: We may have _____ different paths to get here.
I want to share what I've _____ about our generation.

sentences.notes: "the Quad"는 하버드의 Radcliffe Quadrangle 기숙사 지역으로, 메인 캠퍼스에서 멀리 떨어져 있다. "all the way from the Quad"는 비유적으로 "다른 경로"를 의미하면서 동시에 물리적 거리도 가리키는 이중적 유머.

koreanAlignment.en: We may have taken different paths
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 다른 경로를 택했을 수 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: especially if you came all the way from the Quad
but today I want to share
what I've learned about our generation
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 세대에 대해 제가 배운 것을

koreanAlignment.en: and the world we're building together

sentences.original: But first, the last couple of days have brought back a lot of good memories.
sentences.translation: 하지만 먼저, 지난 며칠이 많은 좋은 추억을 떠올려 주었습니다.

vocabulary.example: This song brings back memories of my childhood.
I'll be ready in a couple of minutes.

expressions.usage: 장소, 경험 등이 과거의 기억을 환기시킬 때
본론으로 들어가기 전에 다른 이야기를 꺼낼 때

patterns.explanation: 본론 전에 회상으로 전환하는 패턴. 연설에서 자연스러운 화제 전환에 사용.
examples: But first, let me share something that brought back old memories.
But first, being here has brought back a wave of nostalgia.

drillQuestions.question: The last couple of days have brought _____ a lot of good memories.

sentences.notes: "bring back"은 물리적으로 "가져오다"와 비유적으로 "추억을 되살리다"의 이중 의미. 여기서는 하버드 캠퍼스 방문이 학창 시절 추억을 떠올리게 했다는 의미.

sentences.original: How many of you remember exactly what you were doing when you got that email telling you that you got into Harvard?
sentences.translation: 여러분 중에 하버드에 합격했다는 이메일을 받았을 때 정확히 뭘 하고 있었는지 기억하는 분 몇 명이나 되시나요?

grammarElements.text: telling you that you got into Harvard
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (email을 수식)

vocabulary.example: Tell me exactly what happened.
She worked hard to get into medical school.

expressions.usage: 청중 참여를 유도하는 질문 시작. 손을 들게 하는 상호작용적 표현
대학 입학 합격을 나타내는 구어체 표현

patterns.explanation: 청중에게 공유 경험을 떠올리게 하며 참여를 유도하는 연설 기법.
examples: How many of you remember where you were on that day?
How many of you remember your first day at work?

drillQuestions.question: How many of you remember _____ what you were doing?
You got that email _____ you that you got into Harvard.

sentences.notes: "what you were doing when ..."은 특정 시점의 진행 중인 행동을 묻는 전형적 구문. "got into Harvard"에서 get into는 "입학하다"의 비격식 표현. 청중 참여를 유도하는 수사적 질문.

koreanAlignment.en: telling you that you got into Harvard

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) + 청중 참여 유도 — 공유 경험으로 유대감 형성

sentences.original: I was playing Civilization and I ran downstairs, got my dad, and for some reason, his reaction was to video me opening the email.
sentences.translation: 저는 시빌라이제이션 게임을 하고 있었는데 아래층으로 뛰어 내려가서 아빠를 데려왔고, 어떤 이유에서인지 아빠의 반응은 제가 이메일을 여는 걸 영상으로 찍는 것이었습니다.

grammarElements.text: his reaction was to video me opening the email
grammarElements.label: 주절 (to부정사 보어)

vocabulary.example: Civilization is one of the most popular strategy games.
His reaction to the news was surprising.
She videoed the entire performance.

expressions.usage: 이유를 모르거나 설명할 수 없을 때 사용
급하게 아래층으로 이동할 때

patterns.explanation: 과거진행 상태에서 일련의 빠른 동작들을 나열하는 생동감 있는 서사 패턴.
examples: I was watching TV and I jumped up, grabbed my phone, and called my friend.
I was studying and I heard a noise, looked up, and saw the door open.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ playing Civilization when I got the email.
For some _____, his reaction was to video me.

sentences.notes: "video"가 동사로 사용됨 — "영상을 찍다". "Civilization"은 유명한 PC 전략 게임으로, 이를 언급하는 것이 기술 세대의 공감을 유발. "for some reason"은 아빠의 예상치 못한 반응에 대한 유머.

koreanAlignment.en: and I ran downstairs, got my dad
his reaction was to video me opening the email
koreanAlignment.ko: 아빠의 반응은 제가 이메일을 여는 걸 촬영하는 것이었습니다

sentences.original: That could have been a really sad video.
sentences.translation: 정말 슬픈 영상이 될 뻔했죠.

vocabulary.example: That could have been a disaster.

expressions.usage: 실제로는 일어나지 않았지만 일어날 수 있었던 상황을 가정할 때

patterns.explanation: 과거에 일어나지 않은 가능성을 표현하는 짧은 가정법 패턴. 유머나 안도감 표현에 자주 사용.
examples: That could have been a really embarrassing moment.
That could have been a terrible mistake.

drillQuestions.question: That could _____ been a really sad video.

sentences.notes: "could have been"은 실현되지 않은 과거 가능성을 나타내는 가정법. 만약 불합격했다면 아빠가 촬영한 영상이 슬픈 영상이 되었을 것이라는 뜻. 간결한 문장으로 유머의 여운을 남김.
pronunciationNotes: could have: /ˈkʊdəv/ — "쿠더브", 구어에서 "could've"로 축약됨. "could of"는 오류

sentences.original: I swear getting into Harvard is still the thing my parents are most proud of me for.
sentences.translation: 맹세하건대, 하버드 합격은 아직도 부모님이 저에 대해 가장 자랑스러워하시는 일입니다.

grammarElements.text: the thing my parents are most proud of me for
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계대명사 생략 — thing을 수식)

vocabulary.example: I swear I didn't eat the last cookie.
I'm so proud of what you've accomplished.

expressions.usage: 자신의 말이 사실임을 강조하거나 유머러스하게 과장할 때

expressions.phrase: the thing ... most proud of me for
expressions.usage: proud of 뒤에 이유를 for로 연결

patterns.pattern: [Verb]-ing [X] is still the thing [someone] is most proud of [me/you] for
patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어 + 최상급 proud를 사용하여 가장 자랑스러운 일을 표현하는 패턴.
examples: Graduating college is still the thing my mom is most proud of me for.
Winning that award is still the thing my coach talks about.

drillQuestions.question: Getting into Harvard is still the thing my parents are most _____ of me for.

sentences.notes: 페이스북 창업자인 저커버그가 "하버드 합격"이 부모님이 가장 자랑스러워하는 일이라는 것은 자기비하 유머. "the thing ... for"에서 관계대명사 that이 생략되고, 전치사 for가 문미에 위치(stranded preposition).

koreanAlignment.en: my parents are most proud of me for

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자기비하 유머(self-deprecation) — 세계적 기업가보다 하버드 합격이 부모님의 최고 자랑이라는 아이러니

sentences.original: What about your first lecture at Harvard? Mine was Computer Science 121 with the incredible Harry Lewis.
sentences.translation: 하버드에서의 첫 강의는요? 제 첫 강의는 놀라운 해리 루이스 교수님의 컴퓨터 과학 121이었습니다.

grammarElements.text: What about your first lecture at Harvard
grammarElements.label: 의문문 (화제 전환)

grammarElements.text: Computer Science 121 with the incredible Harry Lewis
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: The professor gave a fascinating lecture on quantum physics.
She did an incredible job on the project.

expressions.usage: 새로운 화제를 제시하거나 상대의 경험을 물을 때
앞서 언급한 명사를 대신하는 소유대명사로 응답할 때

patterns.explanation: 청중에게 같은 경험을 묻고 자신의 경험을 공유하는 대화형 연설 패턴.
examples: What about your first day? Mine was a disaster.
What about your favorite teacher? Mine was Mrs. Johnson.

drillQuestions.question: _____ about your first lecture at Harvard?
_____ was Computer Science 121.

sentences.notes: "What about ...?"는 청중과의 대화적 톤을 유지하는 장치. "Mine"은 "my first lecture"를 대신하는 소유대명사. Harry Lewis는 하버드 컴퓨터공학과의 전설적 교수.

koreanAlignment.en: What about your first lecture at Harvard?

sentences.original: I was late so I threw on a t-shirt and didn't realize until afterwards it was inside out and backwards with my tag sticking out the front.
sentences.translation: 늦어서 티셔츠를 대충 걸치고 갔는데, 나중에야 뒤집어서 거꾸로 입었고 앞쪽으로 태그가 튀어나와 있었다는 걸 깨달았습니다.

grammarElements.text: so I threw on a t-shirt
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (결과 — so)

grammarElements.text: it was inside out and backwards
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (that 생략 — realize의 목적절)

grammarElements.text: with my tag sticking out the front
grammarElements.label: with + 명사 + 현재분사 (부대상황)

vocabulary.example: She threw on a jacket and ran out the door.
Your sweater is inside out.
He put his cap on backwards.
The tag on the shirt was scratchy.

expressions.usage: 서둘러 옷을 입을 때
뒤늦게 사실을 알게 되었을 때
무언가가 밖으로 돌출되어 있을 때

patterns.explanation: "나중에서야 깨달았다"는 의미의 not...until 구문. 뒤늦은 깨달음을 표현.
examples: I didn't realize until afterwards that my zipper was open.
She didn't realize until afterwards that she had the wrong book.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ on a t-shirt because I was late.
I didn't realize _____ afterwards it was inside out.

sentences.notes: "with my tag sticking out"는 "with + 목적어 + 현재분사" 구조로 부대상황(동시 상태)을 나타낸다. throw on은 put on의 더 급한 버전. 생동감 있는 일화로 자신의 어수룩함을 유머로 전환.

koreanAlignment.en: so I threw on a t-shirt
it was inside out and backwards
with my tag sticking out the front

sentences.original: We ended up doing our problem sets together, and now he runs a big part of Facebook.
sentences.translation: 결국 우리는 함께 과제를 하게 되었고, 지금 그는 페이스북의 큰 부분을 운영하고 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: and now he runs a big part of Facebook
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (현재)

vocabulary.example: We ended up staying at the party until midnight.
The professor assigned a challenging problem set.
She runs a successful business.

expressions.usage: 예상치 못하게 어떤 결과에 이르렀을 때

patterns.explanation: 과거의 우연한 시작이 현재의 큰 결과로 이어진 것을 보여주는 서사 패턴.
examples: We ended up sharing an office, and now she runs the entire department.
I ended up helping with the project, and now I lead the team.

drillQuestions.question: We ended _____ doing our problem sets together.
Now he _____ a big part of Facebook.

sentences.notes: "end up doing"은 계획하지 않았던 결과를 나타내는 관용 표현. "problem sets"는 미국 대학(특히 이공계)에서 과제를 뜻하는 용어. 작은 우정이 큰 결과로 이어진 이야기.

sentences.original: And that, Class of 2017, is why you should be nice to people.
sentences.translation: 그리고 바로 그것이, 2017년 졸업생 여러분, 여러분이 사람들에게 친절해야 하는 이유입니다.

grammarElements.text: why you should be nice to people
grammarElements.label: 보어 (why 관계부사절)

vocabulary.example: You should always be nice to your colleagues.

expressions.usage: 이야기의 교훈을 극적으로 전달할 때. 주어와 동사 사이에 호격을 삽입하여 극적 효과를 높임

patterns.pattern: And that, [audience], is why you should [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 일화를 마무리하며 교훈을 전달하는 펀치라인 패턴. 삽입 호격이 극적 일시정지를 만듦.
examples: And that, my friends, is why you should never give up.
And that, ladies and gentlemen, is why preparation matters.

drillQuestions.question: And that, Class of 2017, _____ why you should be nice to people.

sentences.notes: 주어(that)와 동사(is) 사이에 "Class of 2017"을 삽입하여 극적인 일시정지(dramatic pause)를 만드는 수사법. 작은 일화(티셔츠를 뒤집어 입은 날 친절했던 친구)에서 보편적 교훈을 끌어내는 연설 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: is why you should be nice to people

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 극적 일시정지(dramatic pause) + 교훈 전달(moral) — 삽입 호격이 펀치라인의 임팩트를 극대화

sentences.original: But my best memory from Harvard was meeting Priscilla.
sentences.translation: 하지만 하버드에서의 가장 좋은 기억은 프리실라를 만난 것입니다.

vocabulary.example: My best memory from college is meeting my best friend.

expressions.usage: 특정 장소에서의 최고의 경험을 회상할 때

patterns.explanation: 특정 장소에서의 최고 경험을 동명사로 표현하는 회상 패턴.
examples: My best memory from high school was winning the championship.
My best memory from that trip was watching the sunset.

drillQuestions.question: My best memory from Harvard _____ meeting Priscilla.

sentences.notes: 간결하지만 감동적인 전환 문장. 여러 하버드 추억 중 가장 좋은 것이 아내를 만난 것이라는 고백. 동명사 "meeting"이 보어로 사용.

koreanAlignment.en: But my best memory from Harvard

sentences.original: As luck would have it, Priscilla was at that party with her friend.
sentences.translation: 운이 좋게도, 프리실라가 그 파티에 친구와 함께 와 있었습니다.

vocabulary.example: It was just pure luck that I found it.

expressions.usage: 우연의 일치를 소개할 때 사용하는 관용 표현. 문맥에 따라 행운/불운 모두 가능

patterns.explanation: 우연의 일치를 소개하는 격식 있는 관용 패턴. 이야기에 운명적 느낌을 부여.
examples: As luck would have it, the train was delayed and I met my old friend.
As luck would have it, there was one seat left.

drillQuestions.question: As luck _____ have it, Priscilla was at that party.

sentences.notes: "As luck would have it"은 격식 있는 관용 표현으로, "우연히, 공교롭게도"라는 뜻. 여기서는 "운 좋게도"의 긍정적 의미. would have it은 가정법 형태이지만 관용적으로 굳어진 표현.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 운명적 우연(serendipity) — 관용구를 사용해 만남에 운명적 분위기를 부여

sentences.original: Actually, any of you graduating can use that line.
sentences.translation: 사실, 졸업하는 여러분 누구나 그 대사를 써도 됩니다.

vocabulary.example: That was a great line from the movie.
The graduating class celebrated together.

expressions.usage: 누군가의 말을 인용하거나 따라 사용할 때. 특히 연애 멘트에 대해

patterns.explanation: 청중 전체에게 제안이나 허락을 주는 유머러스한 패턴.
examples: Actually, any of you leaving early can take some food.
Actually, any of you interested can sign up online.

drillQuestions.question: Any of you _____ can use that line.

sentences.notes: 직전에 프리실라에게 했던 대사("3일 후에 퇴학당하니까 빨리 데이트해야 해요")를 졸업생들도 쓸 수 있다는 유머. 졸업 = 떠남 = 퇴학이라는 유사점을 이용한 재치.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 청중 참여 유머(audience callback) — 앞선 일화의 대사를 청중에게 적용하는 콜백 유머

sentences.original: Today I want to talk about purpose. But I'm not here to give you the standard commencement about finding your purpose.
sentences.translation: 오늘 저는 목적에 대해 이야기하고 싶습니다. 하지만 여러분의 목적을 찾는 것에 대한 전형적인 졸업식 연설을 하려고 여기 온 것은 아닙니다.

grammarElements.text: I'm not here to give you
grammarElements.label: 주절 (부정 — 목적 부정)

grammarElements.text: the standard commencement about finding your purpose
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (동명사구 수식)

vocabulary.example: Finding your purpose in life is important.
The commencement ceremony was held outdoors.
This is the standard procedure for handling complaints.

expressions.usage: 청중의 예상을 부정하며 다른 방향을 제시할 때
흔한 졸업식 연설의 클리셰를 가리킬 때

patterns.pattern: I want to talk about [X]. But I'm not here to [typical approach]. Instead, ...
patterns.explanation: 주제를 제시한 뒤, 흔한 접근을 부정하고 자신만의 관점을 예고하는 연설 구조 패턴.
examples: I want to talk about success. But I'm not here to tell you to follow your dreams.
I want to talk about education. But I'm not here to repeat the same old advice.

drillQuestions.question: I'm not here to _____ you the standard commencement.
Today I want to talk about _____.

sentences.notes: "commencement"는 "시작"이라는 원래 뜻에서 "졸업식"이라는 의미로 발전. 졸업이 새로운 시작이라는 뜻. "the standard commencement"는 졸업식 연설의 클리셰(꿈을 찾으라)를 의식적으로 거부하는 메타적 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: Today I want to talk about purpose
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘 저는 목적에 대해 이야기하고 싶습니다

koreanAlignment.en: But I'm not here to give you

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 기대 전복(subversion of expectation) — 전형적 졸업 연설의 클리셰를 의도적으로 거부하며 독자적 논지를 예고

sentences.original: The challenge for our generation is creating a world where everyone has a sense of purpose.
sentences.translation: 우리 세대의 과제는 모든 사람이 목적 의식을 갖는 세상을 만드는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: where everyone has a sense of purpose
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (where — world를 수식)

vocabulary.example: Climate change is the greatest challenge of our time.
Our generation must tackle new problems.
Volunteering gives people a sense of purpose.

expressions.usage: 세대적 책임과 사명을 언급할 때
삶의 방향성과 의미를 가리키는 핵심 표현

patterns.pattern: The challenge for [our generation / our time] is [verb]-ing a world where ...
patterns.explanation: 세대적 사명을 선언하는 패턴. 동명사로 과제를 표현하고 where절로 이상적 세상을 묘사.
examples: The challenge for our time is building a society where everyone has access to education.
The challenge for this generation is creating an economy where no one is left behind.

drillQuestions.question: The challenge for our generation _____ creating a world where everyone has purpose.
Everyone should have a _____ of purpose.

sentences.notes: 연설의 핵심 테제(thesis). "finding your purpose"(개인적 목적 찾기)가 아니라 "creating a world where everyone has a sense of purpose"(모든 사람이 목적을 갖는 세상 만들기)라는 더 큰 비전을 제시. 이 문장이 이후 연설 전체의 구조를 결정한다.

koreanAlignment.en: where everyone has a sense of purpose

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 테제 선언(thesis statement) — 연설의 핵심 주장을 명확하게 선언하여 이후 논의의 방향을 설정
